name: cpt-datastore
summary: Full CPT-backed entity stack: model, adapter, datastore, registrar, and WordPress-backed handler in one shot.
description: Creates a complete WordPress-backed datastore stack: model, model adapter, datastore (interface + handler interface + implementation), the post type registrar, and a CPT-backed handler that translates the typed datastore into wp_insert_post / WP_Query / wp_update_post / wp_delete_post calls.
problem: You're starting a new entity that lives in WordPress posts and you want the entire idiomatic PHPNomad stack scaffolded in one shot — typed model, registered post type, datastore interface and implementation, and the CPT-backed handler that ties them together — without copying boilerplate from another plugin.
appliesWhen: You're starting a new entity that will live in WordPress as a custom post type
You want the conventional PHPNomad data layer end-to-end with no gaps
Consumers should access the entity through a typed datastore, not WP_Query directly
The data is post-shaped enough that posts + post meta is a sensible store
You want one command that produces a working baseline you can flesh out
avoidWhen: You only need the post type registered — use phpnomad/cpt instead
You already have a datastore and only need a CPT-backed handler — use phpnomad/cpt-handler
The data is genuinely relational and not post-shaped — use phpnomad/database-datastore against a custom table
You want WordPress code to interact via WP_Query directly with no datastore — use phpnomad/cpt

general: full data layer for a CPT
wordpress: custom post type with data layer

examples: Set up everything I need to store events in WordPress as a CPT
Scaffold a full data layer for portfolio items backed by a post type
Bootstrap CRUD for testimonials with a custom post type
Create the entire data stack for an entity stored as posts
tradeoffs: Generates seven files plus DI bindings and a Ready listener. Fast to bootstrap, but you'll be editing TODOs (model fields, adapter translations, post-vs-meta field map). WordPress meta_query is convenient but slow at scale — if performance matters use phpnomad/database-datastore instead.
outputs: A {name}Datastore class plus its public and handler interfaces
A {name}CptDatastoreHandler implementing the handler interface against WordPress
DI bindings tying it all together plus a Ready listener for the registrar
postApply: Customize POST_FIELDS in the handler — decide which model fields are post columns vs post meta
Customize the register_post_type() args in the registrar (capabilities, supports, taxonomies, etc.)
Smoke test reads and writes through the datastore inside WordPress
Run `wp post-type list` (WP-CLI) to confirm the CPT is registered

name.description: Entity name shared across model, adapter, datastore, registrar, and handler (PascalCase, no suffix)
name.aiHint: Singular PascalCase. Same name flows through every generated file.

postType.description: WordPress post type slug (lowercase, dash-separated, max 20 chars)
postType.aiHint: lowercase with optional dashes. Should be prefixed by the plugin slug to avoid collisions (e.g. acme-order, not order). Max 20 chars per WordPress.

pluralLabel.description: Plural display name shown in admin UI

singularLabel.description: Singular display name shown in admin UI

textDomain.description: Plugin text domain for label translations

initializer.description: FQCN of the initializer to register DI bindings and the listener in
initializer.aiHint: For a plugin scaffolded via phpnomad/wp-plugin, use {namespace}\AppInit.
